steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content:
Sentence 1: Switzerland lowered the voting age from 20 to 18. Sentence 2: Many people gained a new opportunity to participate in politics. The second sentence is a direct consequence of the first — the age reduction caused the expanded participation. The transition must signal a cause-and-effect relationship.
  • Choice A — 'Nevertheless,': Signals something happens despite the previous statement — but gaining participation is not despite the age reduction; it is caused by it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'As a result,': Directly signals that what follows is a consequence of what came before, matching the causal relationship. ✓
  • Choice C — 'By contrast,': Signals opposition or contrast, but expanded participation is not in contrast to lowering the voting age. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Similarly,': Signals that something is like something else, but the second sentence is a consequence, not a parallel situation. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: "As a result" is the only transition that correctly expresses that more people gained voting rights as a direct consequence of Switzerland lowering the voting age. That matches choice B — B is correct.
